name: pledge-skills
description: Comprehensive agent skills for Astro, Bun, Express, Go, Next.js, Python, React, React Native, Redis, Ruby on Rails, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Rust, OXC, Vite, Zig, and Kotlin.

skills.name: express-docs
skills.description: Express.js 5.x — getting started, routing, middleware, error handling, Application/Request/Response/Router API, template engines, debugging, database integration, security, performance, production patterns, and migration guides.

skills.name: astro-docs
skills.description: Astro 5.x — components, pages, routing, layouts, styling, content collections, islands architecture, server islands, actions, middleware, endpoints, data fetching, view transitions, integrations, deployment, and configuration.

skills.name: nextjs-docs
skills.description: Next.js 16.2.7 with React 19.2 — App Router, Server Components, caching, deployment, and all modern patterns.

skills.name: react-docs
skills.description: React 19 reference — hooks, concurrent features, Server Components, React Compiler, Suspense, and advanced patterns.

skills.name: typescript-docs
skills.description: TypeScript 5.x — types, generics, narrowing, TSConfig, JSX, decorators, declaration files, and migration.

skills.name: tailwindcss-docs
skills.description: Tailwind CSS v4 — utility classes, responsive design, customization, v4 CSS-first config, and all utilities.

skills.name: zig-docs
skills.description: Zig 0.16.0 — primitive types, arrays, slices, pointers, structs, enums, unions, control flow, functions, errors, optionals, casting, comptime, builtins, memory management, build system, C interop, standard library, testing, targets, and style guide.

skills.name: react-native-docs
skills.description: React Native 0.86 — core components, APIs, styling, flexbox, navigation, networking, animations, platform-specific code, debugging, performance, TypeScript, testing, publishing, New Architecture (Fabric, JSI, TurboModules, Hermes), and security.

skills.name: rust-docs
skills.description: Rust 1.97.0 (Edition 2024) — ownership, borrowing, lifetimes, types, control flow, functions, closures, structs, enums, traits, generics, error handling, collections, strings, concurrency, async/await, modules, macros, smart pointers, unsafe Rust, FFI, standard library, testing, and Cargo.

skills.name: oxc-docs
skills.description: JavaScript Oxidation Compiler (Oxc) — high-performance JavaScript tools in Rust. Covers parser design, lexer, AST, semantic analysis, architecture, linter, formatter, transformer, minifier, resolver, ECMAScript specification, and performance optimization.

skills.name: redis-docs
skills.description: Redis 8.x — getting started, all data types (strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets, streams, JSON, geospatial, probabilistic, time series, vector sets, arrays), pub/sub, keyspace notifications, commands reference, client libraries (Python, JavaScript, Java, C#, PHP), keys and patterns, persistence (RDB, AOF), security (ACL, TLS), clustering, replication, sentinel, performance tuning, AI vector search, full-text search and query, Lua scripting and functions, monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog), deployment (Redis Cloud, Kubernetes, Docker), RIOT data tool, and full configuration reference.

skills.name: vite-docs
skills.description: Vite 8.x (Rolldown + Oxc) — getting started, philosophy, features (HMR, CSS, TypeScript, JSX, Web Workers, WASM), CLI, plugins, dependency pre-bundling, static assets, production build (chunking, multi-page, library mode), deployment (GitHub Pages, Netlify, Vercel, Cloudflare), env variables and modes, SSR, backend integration, troubleshooting, performance, migration from v7, Plugin API, HMR API, JavaScript API, full config reference, and Environment API.

skills.name: npm-docs
skills.description: npm 12.x — about npm, registry, packages and modules, scopes, public/private packages, user accounts, 2FA, profile, billing, local environment setup, package.json fields, dependencies (dependencies, devDependencies, peerDependencies, optionalDependencies), semantic versioning, Node.js modules, creating and publishing unscoped/scoped/private/org packages, dist-tags, managing published packages (updating, deprecating, transferring, unpublishing, visibility, collaborators), installing packages (searching, local/global, updating, uninstalling, EACCES), security (audit, provenance, OIDC, staged publishing, ECDSA signatures, 2FA, malware), integrations (access tokens, CI/CD, Docker, private registries), organizations (creating, managing, billing, members, teams, org packages), full CLI commands reference (all npm commands), configuring npm (.npmrc, package-lock.json, folders, install), using npm (registry, package spec, config, logging, scope, scripts, workspaces, dependency selectors), troubleshooting (common errors, debug logs), and policies (terms, conduct, disputes, license, privacy, unpublish, DMCA, security).

skills.name: django-docs
skills.description: Django 6.0 — getting started, models (fields, relationships, Meta options, inheritance, composite PKs), model field reference (all field types, options, validators), QuerySets (filtering, Q objects, F expressions, aggregation, raw SQL, async queries), migrations (operations, data migrations, squashing), views (function-based, class-based, decorators, shortcuts, async views), URL routing (URLconfs, path converters, reverse, namespaces), templates (DTL syntax, tags, filters, inheritance, custom tags), forms (Form, ModelForm, formsets, validation, widgets), admin site (ModelAdmin, inlines, actions, customization), authentication (users, groups, permissions, backends, password management, custom user model), middleware (built-in, custom, async, hooks), security (CSRF, XSS, SQL injection, clickjacking, CSP, SSL/HTTPS), testing (TestCase, Client, fixtures, async testing), caching (Redis, Memcached, database, file, per-view, low-level API, async cache), settings reference, deployment (WSGI, ASGI, Docker, Nginx, static files), internationalization (i18n, localization, time zones), async support (async views, ORM, middleware, cache), signals, sessions, messages, pagination, performance optimization, file uploads and storage, REST API patterns (DRF, serializers, viewsets, JWT), and breaking changes from 5.x to 6.0.

skills.name: kotlin-docs
skills.description: Kotlin 2.4.0 — variables, basic types, strings, control flow, ranges, when expressions, functions, lambdas, function types, inline functions, reified type parameters, classes, constructors, properties, inheritance, interfaces, abstract classes, data classes, sealed classes, enums, objects, companion objects, delegation, generics (variance, reified, star projections), collections (List, Set, Map, sequences, ArrayDeque), null safety (nullable types, safe calls, Elvis, smart casts), coroutines (suspend, launch, async, Flow, StateFlow, SharedFlow, channels, dispatchers, structured concurrency, cancellation, exception handling), DSLs, type-safe builders, scope functions (let, run, with, apply, also), extension functions, property delegation, reflection (KClass, callable references, property/constructor references, Java reflection interop), annotations (declaration, use-site targets, @Target, @Retention, @Repeatable, Java annotations), exceptions (throw, try-catch-finally, precondition functions, Nothing type, custom exceptions, exception hierarchy), type casts and equality (is/!is, as/as?, smart casts, structural vs referential equality, floating-point equality, array equality), Kotlin Multiplatform (KMP, expect/actual, platform targets, Compose Multiplatform), serialization (@Serializable, JSON, ProtoBuf, polymorphism, custom serializers), Java interoperability (interop, migration, JSpecify, @JvmStatic, @JvmField, @JvmOverloads), testing (JUnit 5, parameterized tests, coroutine testing, Flow testing, MockK), build tools (Gradle Kotlin DSL, Maven, KSP, kapt, compiler options), idiomatic patterns, coding conventions, best practices, and what's new in 2.4.0.

skills.name: go-docs
skills.description: Go 1.26 — getting started, tutorials, language specification (syntax, types, functions, structs, interfaces, generics, self-referential type constraints), effective Go idioms, concurrency (goroutines, channels, select, sync, atomic, context, memory model), standard library (fmt, strings, bytes, bufio, strconv, unicode, errors, log/slog, cmp, iter, io, os, path/filepath, embed, net, net/http, net/url, net/netip, crypto/aes, crypto/ecdsa, crypto/ed25519, crypto/rsa, crypto/sha*, crypto/tls, crypto/x509, crypto/hpke, crypto/mlkem, crypto/fips140, encoding/json v1+v2, encoding/xml, encoding/base64, encoding/binary, encoding/csv, encoding/gob, container/heap, container/list, sort, slices, maps, math, math/big, regexp, hash, unique, weak, os/exec, os/signal, runtime, runtime/debug, runtime/metrics, runtime/pprof, runtime/trace, plugin, syscall, time, flag, expvar, testing, testing/synctest, fuzzing, coverage, benchmarks, text/template, html/template, mime, image, go/ast, go/parser, go/types, go/format, go/doc, reflect), modules (go.mod, versioning, publishing, workspaces, dependencies, project layout), database/sql (transactions, prepared statements, connection pooling, drivers), tools (go command, gofmt, pprof, trace, diagnostics, PGO, GC guide, editors, cross-compilation, Docker), and what's new in Go 1.26 (Green Tea GC, new() with expression, crypto/hpke, simd/archsimd, runtime/secret, heap randomization).

skills.name: swr-docs
skills.description: SWR 2.3 — React data fetching library with built-in caching, revalidation (focus, interval, reconnect), mutation (useSWRMutation, optimistic updates, rollback, populateCache), pagination (useSWRInfinite, index/cursor, parallel fetching), prefetching (preload, fallback, RSC), cache providers, middleware (logger, laggy, serialize), suspense mode, subscription (useSWRSubscription, WebSocket, Firestore), error handling (status codes, retry, global error report), TypeScript support (type inference, generics, Fetcher, middleware types), and Next.js integration (App Router, RSC, SSG/SSR, fallback, complex keys).

skills.name: python-docs
skills.description: Python 3.13 — variables, built-in types, strings, f-strings, control flow, pattern matching, functions, lambda, *args/**kwargs, decorators, generators, closures, classes, inheritance, dataclasses, enums, metaclasses, properties, slots, dunder methods, abstract base classes, collections (list, dict, set, tuple, comprehensions, Counter, defaultdict, deque, OrderedDict, ChainMap), modules and packages, imports, pip, venv, pyproject.toml, packaging, exceptions, try/except/else/finally, exception groups (except*), context managers, warnings, type hints (generics, protocols, TypedDict, TypeVar, overloading, narrowing, mypy, pyright), async/await, asyncio (tasks, TaskGroup, streams, synchronization, async generators, timeouts, event loop), standard library (os, sys, math, itertools, functools, datetime, json, re, argparse, random, pathlib, io, csv, pickle, logging, shutil, tempfile, subprocess), testing (pytest, unittest, mocking, fixtures, markers, parametrize, coverage, doctest, hypothesis), idiomatic patterns, PEP 8, performance tips, EAFP, duck typing, and what's new in Python 3.13 (free-threaded CPython, JIT compiler, improved REPL, better error messages, PEP 649 lazy annotations, TypeIs), 3.12 (PEP 695 generics, f-string formalization, comprehension inlining, @override), and 3.11 (exception groups, fine-grained tracebacks, exception notes, faster CPython, Self type).

skills.name: rails-docs
skills.description: Ruby on Rails 8.1 — getting started, Active Record (migrations, validations, callbacks, associations, query interface, Active Model), Action View (layouts, rendering, helpers, form helpers), Action Controller (parameters, sessions, cookies, CSRF, streaming), routing, Active Support (core extensions, instrumentation), Action Mailer/Mailbox/Text, Active Job (Solid Queue, continuations), Active Storage (uploads, variants, direct uploads), Action Cable (WebSockets, Turbo Streams), I18n, testing (Minitest, fixtures, system tests), security (CSRF, SQL injection, XSS, authentication), caching (Solid Cache, fragment, Russian doll), API-only apps, configuration (Propshaft, importmaps, autoloading, initialization), debugging, performance and threading, advanced Active Record (PostgreSQL, multiple databases, encryption, composite primary keys), extending Rails (plugins, Rack, generators, engines), and upgrading (Rails 8.1/8.0/7.2 release notes).

skills.name: bun-docs
skills.description: Bun 1.2 — all-in-one JS/TS toolkit: runtime (bun run, watch mode, REPL, bunfig.toml, file types, JSX, module resolution, auto-install, plugins), package manager (bun install, add, remove, workspaces, lockfile, overrides, global packages, production mode), HTTP server (Bun.serve, routing, HTML imports, WebSockets, TLS, HTTP/3, cookies), APIs (Bun.file, Bun.write, bun:sqlite, streams, fetch, workers, Bun.spawn, Bun.shell, hashing, glob, HTMLRewriter, TOML/YAML/Markdown parsing), advanced APIs (SQL PostgreSQL/MySQL, S3 object storage, Redis, TCP/UDP sockets, DNS, cron, WebView, FFI, C compiler, transpiler, image processing, archive/tar, binary data, cookies, CSRF, JSON5/JSONL, Node.js compatibility), test runner (bun test, assertions, mocks, snapshots, watch mode, lifecycle hooks, concurrent tests, coverage), and bundler (bun build, targets, plugins, code splitting, minify, sourcemap, single-file executables, bytecode, HTML imports).
